# Documentation-Quality Checklist: Roadmap-MOC home note from PRD + coach the two-zone structure

**Purpose**: Validate that PRSG-004's documentation requirements are complete, clear, consistent, and unambiguous — specifically the two-zone home-note model, the prd-emit/coach-teach no-drift agreement, the relative-link/wikilink rules, the render_index context-scoping, and Codex mirror parity. Unit tests for the requirements, not the implementation.
**Created**: 2026-06-08
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Two-Zone Delineation (curated vs sentinel-bounded GENERATED INDEX)

- [ ] CHK001 - Are the two zones of the home note named and distinguished with non-overlapping definitions (human-curated epics zone vs sentinel-bounded GENERATED INDEX zone)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002, §Key Entities]
- [ ] CHK002 - Is the boundary mechanism for the GENERATED INDEX zone specified as a sentinel pair (not prose, not heading) so a reader knows exactly where the generated region begins and ends? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, Contract §"Zone framing & assembly"]
- [ ] CHK003 - Is it stated unambiguously that the curated zone is hand-authored/editable while the GENERATED INDEX zone is machine-regenerated and MUST NOT be hand-edited? [Clarity, Spec §FR-009, §Key Entities]
- [ ] CHK004 - Does the spec specify that the home note carries ONLY the INDEX sentinel pair (not the PRS or BACKLINKS pairs), and is the consequence of that choice stated? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, Contract §"Zone framing & assembly"]
- [ ] CHK005 - Is the requirement that `speckit-prd` emits the home note as a SEPARATE file (not zones injected into the prose technical-roadmap) stated explicitly? [Clarity, Spec §FR-001]
- [ ] CHK006 - Are the contents of the curated zone (epic title, member spec links, one-line advisory "Why") enumerated so the zone's shape is unambiguous? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003, §Key Entities]
- [ ] CHK007 - Does the plan's modified `roadmap-moc-template.md` description make clear which zone the template ships (the empty INDEX sentinel pair) and which zone is filled by prd at emit time? [Consistency, Plan §"Declared File Operations", §"Project Structure"]

## prd Emit (US1) vs coach Teach (US2) — No-Drift Agreement

- [ ] CHK008 - Do the US1 (prd emit) and US2 (coach teach) requirements reference the SAME two-zone model (same zone names, same curated/generated semantics) rather than two independently worded descriptions? [Consistency, Spec §FR-002, §FR-009]
- [x] CHK009 - Do the coach teaching and the prd emission conform to a single definition of the two-zone structure (rather than two independent descriptions that merely happen to agree)? [Consistency, Spec §Key Entities, §FR-002, §FR-009] — PASS: the two-zone model is defined once in Key Entities (lines 269-275); FR-002 (emit) and FR-009 (teach) both conform to that single definition, so agreement is by construction. An anti-drift maintenance mechanism is out of documentation-quality scope.
- [ ] CHK010 - Is the "cap epics below ~10" guardrail described consistently between the prd emit-time advisory (US1) and the coach teaching (US2) — same threshold, same advisory-not-block framing? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005, §FR-010]
- [ ] CHK011 - Is the advisory (warn, never block, never a CI lint) nature of the epic cap stated identically wherever it appears (US1 emit, US2 teach, Success Criteria)? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005, §FR-010, §SC-003]
- [ ] CHK012 - Does the spec specify WHERE the coach two-zone teaching lives (the dedicated reference doc) so its content location is unambiguous and singular? [Clarity, Spec §Assumptions "coach teaching location", Plan §"Project Structure"]

## Relative-Link & Path Rules (never wikilinks; exact relative path)

- [ ] CHK013 - Is the rule that every INDEX link is a relative `[]()` Markdown target and NEVER a `[[wikilink]]` stated explicitly? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014, §SC-006, Contract §"Row format"]
- [ ] CHK014 - Is the exact relative path `../../../specs/<dir>/SPEC-MOC.md` specified (with the rationale: home note in `docs/ai/specs/`, spec tree in repo-root `specs/`) so the link depth is unambiguous? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014, Contract §"Row format"]
- [ ] CHK015 - Is the home note's own frontmatter `up:` link rule specified as a relative `[]()` target (pointing at the technical-roadmap), consistent with the never-wikilink rule applied to the INDEX rows? [Consistency, Spec §FR-006]
- [ ] CHK016 - Is the exact INDEX row byte format pinned (`- [<spec_id>](rel/SPEC-MOC.md) · <status>`), including that the separator is the U+00B7 MIDDLE DOT and not an ASCII dot? [Completeness, Spec §FR-012, Contract §"Row format (exact)"]
- [ ] CHK017 - Is the empty/missing-status row form pinned to exact bytes (separator present, status blank, row not dropped) so the never-wikilink/relative-link rule holds even on the edge row? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-015, Contract §"Empty / missing status"]

## render_index Context-Scoping (home note ONLY; spec-MOC INDEX stays empty)

- [ ] CHK018 - Is it stated unambiguously that `render_index` emits repo-wide rows ONLY when the target is the roadmap-MOC home note, and renders empty for every spec-MOC? [Clarity, Spec §FR-011, §FR-018]
- [ ] CHK019 - Does the spec document that `render_index` is already invoked against every spec-MOC's present INDEX zone today (so activation must branch, not unconditionally emit)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-018, Research §"Decision 1"]
- [ ] CHK020 - Is the byte-identical guarantee for the per-spec-MOC code path (so the pinned PRSG-003 contracts stay green) stated as a hard requirement and tied to a success criterion? [Consistency, Spec §FR-018, §SC-005]
- [ ] CHK021 - Does the PLAN (not only the spec/research) state the context-scoping unambiguously — that the home-note signal originates at discovery in `main()` and the spec-MOC INDEX continues to render empty? [Consistency, Plan §"Summary"]
- [ ] CHK022 - Is the home-note discovery mechanism specified (filename glob `docs/ai/specs/*-roadmap-MOC.md`, gated via `moc_is_gated`, disjoint from the `specs/` scan) so the "in addition to and disjoint from" boundary is unambiguous? [Completeness, Spec §FR-017, Contract §"Home-note location & discovery"]
- [ ] CHK023 - Is the repo-wide scope of the home-note INDEX (one row per gated spec under a single roadmap) stated together with its limitation (multi-roadmap per-roadmap scoping deferred to PRSG-011)? [Clarity, Spec §Assumptions, Research §"Decision 5"]
- [ ] CHK024 - Are the determinism/idempotence requirements (zero-byte second-run diff) specified for the home-note INDEX path? [Measurability, Spec §FR-019, §SC-004]

## Codex Mirror Parity (mirrors say exactly what the Claude variants say)

- [x] CHK025 - Does the spec require the Codex `speckit-prd` and `speckit-coach` mirrors to convey the SAME content/meaning as the Claude variants, rather than only requiring the prose to "exist in both" mirrors? [Resolved, Spec §FR-020, §SC-008] — was a gap (FR-020/SC-008 originally required only co-presence: "exist in BOTH" / "appear in both"); now FR-020 and SC-008 require semantic equivalence (same two-zone model, emit step, and teaching), allowing each runtime's own voice and interview mechanism.
- [ ] CHK026 - Is the parity standard measurable — does the spec define what "parity" requires (equivalent two-zone model + emit step + teaching), so a reviewer can objectively decide whether the mirrors match? [Measurability, Spec §FR-020, §SC-008]
- [ ] CHK027 - Is the boundary of what must be mirrored vs what may legitimately differ (e.g., Codex free-text Q&A loop vs `AskUserQuestion`) stated so "say exactly the same thing" is not contradicted by the interview-mechanism difference? [Clarity, Spec §Assumptions "Codex parity", Research §"Decision 7"]
- [ ] CHK028 - Is the single-shared-generator rule (the generator is referenced by path and NOT duplicated into the Codex tree) stated explicitly and distinguished from the prose-mirroring requirement? [Consistency, Spec §FR-021, §SC-008]
- [ ] CHK029 - Is the no-duplicate-doc arrangement for the coach reference (Codex coach mirror links to the shared `references/` tree) documented so parity is achieved without a divergent second copy? [Completeness, Spec §Assumptions "coach teaching location", Research §"Decision 7"]

## Cross-Cutting Requirement Quality

- [ ] CHK030 - Is the new-roadmaps-only / no-backfill boundary stated unambiguously, with the deferred owner named (PRSG-011)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007, §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK031 - Are the no-phases fallback (single "Specs" epic + advisory) requirements documented so the curated-zone shape is defined for the flat-catalog case? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-004, §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK032 - Does every functional requirement carry a user-story tag and does the FR→US mapping resolve without ambiguity? [Traceability, Spec §"Functional Requirements"]
- [ ] CHK033 - Is the deliberate test-coverage deviation (adding Layer 4 to the catalog's L1) documented with its rationale so the deviation is auditable? [Consistency, Spec §"Test Coverage Note", §SC-004, §SC-005]
- [ ] CHK034 - Are the reciprocal-link requirements (home note `up:` → roadmap AND roadmap → home note) stated on both sides so mutual reachability is unambiguous? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006]

## Notes

- This checklist tests the QUALITY of PRSG-004's documentation/requirements (completeness, clarity, consistency, measurability), not the behavior of the generator or skills.
- Each item references a spec section, plan section, contract section, research decision, or a gap/ambiguity marker for traceability.
- Gap-marked items flag a requirement-quality concern that may need a spec/plan edit before the documentation is considered drift-proof. Two items were authored as gap candidates: CHK025 was a genuine gap and is remediated (FR-020/SC-008 tightened to require semantic equivalence of the Codex mirrors); CHK009 was investigated and reclassified as PASS (agreement-by-construction via the single Key Entities definition — no edit needed). No open gap markers remain.
